description: Main processes simulated (additions only) Within FESOM 2.7, FESIM 2.7 gains tighter coupling to an explicit iceberg drift-and-melt module (with wave-induced erosion), so that calved icebergs are treated as an external freshwater/heat source distinct from the resolved sea-ice cover. Consistent handling of land-ice and ice-shelf freshwater fluxes alongside sea-ice melt/growth is hardened, including the cold-start pathway used for transient passive tracers (CFCs, SF6, radiocarbon) and water isotopes (δ18O, δD) that interact with the ice–ocean interface.  Key physics / parameterisations (new or upgraded) Continued use of the modified elastic–viscous–plastic (mEVP) rheology on collocated triangular vertex meshes, with subcycle counts exposed via namelist. Flux-corrected transport advection of ice and snow mean thicknesses and concentration, unchanged in formulation but cleaned up at the interface to the restructured CVmix vertical-mixing layer of the host ocean. Improved ice–ocean–ice-shelf cavity consistency: rigid-lid/free-surface treatment under cavities, water-flux closure at the ice base, and a vertical-velocity reset that participates in the CFLz check. Calving/enthalpy logic reworked for stability under frequent restarts in coupled IFS-based configurations.  Notable features of this version First-phase OpenACC port: the FESIM dynamical kernels run alongside the FESOM 2.7 dynamical core on GPUs while retaining the existing MPI CPU path. CMIP7-oriented sea-ice diagnostics exposed via the ldiag_cmor diagnostic layer, with 0-dimensional global metrics written one file per simulated year. Asynchronous parallel sea-ice output through the MultIO 2.6 back-end, in addition to the native and XIOS paths. Coupled-system robustness improvements relevant to sea ice: corrected wind coupling at the ice surface, reproducibility under frequent restarts, and reduced coupling memory footprint. Build & test infrastructure: ecbundle support, multi-environment CI, and ctest regression tests that exercise cavity and iceberg setups together with the sea-ice component.
